  • Round 2 Cutoff for B.Tech is 65-5137 for the General Category.

25 May, 2026 CEED 2027 exam date announced. Exam on Jan 17, 2027. Detailed information will be released by Oct 1, 2026 @ceed.iitb.ac.in. Read news!

22 May, 2026 IIT Bombay Placements 2024-25 concluded with 1,422 offers from 417 companies, achieving a 70% placement rate. The Average CTC stood at INR 26.45 LPA, while the median salary stood at INR 20 LPA. Read more.

22 May, 2026 IIT Bombay also announced Fee structure 2026 for UG programs. The total academic fee is ₹ 11.96 Lakhs-₹ 14.87 Lakhs. Read more.

IIT Bombay Fee structure 2026 has been released for PG programs. The total academic fee is ₹ 2.66 Lakhs-₹ 45 Lakhs. Learn more.

IIT Bombay Doctorate Fee Structure 2026 has been released. The total academic fee is ₹ 3.59 Lakhs. Read news.

Indian Institute of Technology Bombay, popularly known as IIT Bombay or IIT Mumbai, is an autonomous institute established in 1958. As per the data, the maximum number of top JEE (Advanced) rankers chose IIT Bombay because of its career opportunities and its location in the financial hub of India. IITB Ranking is 3rd in the B.Tech. category by NIRF 2025. According to Collegedunia Rankings, the institute has been ranked 1st for B.Tech.

IITB offers B.Tech as one of the most popular programs. Admission to B.Tech is based on JEE Advanced scores, followed by JoSAA Counselling. IIT Bombay JEE Advanced Cutoff Rank 2025 Round 1 for the General Category is 66 - 4696.

As per IIT Bombay Placements, the average package was INR 23.5 LPA. 22 students accepted offers above INR 1 Cr PA. 388 companies participated, including Fortune 500 companies. The top recruiters at IIT Bombay Placement included Airbus, Apple, Google, Intel, Godrej, Shell, Halliburton, Accenture, Barclays, Michelin, Cipla, Abbott, Pfizer, Amazon, Honeywell, Hitachi, Boeing, and Reliance.

IIT Bombay Admission 2026 Dates

Here are the important dates of the admission process conducted by IIT Bombay.

IIT Bombay B.Tech Admission Dates 2026

IIT Bombay accepts JEE Advanced scores followed by JoSAA Counselling for admission to the B.Tech program. Candidates can check the schedule of both the exams and counselling are tabulated below:

IIT Bombay M.Tech Admission Dates 2026

M.Tech admission at IIT Bombay is based on a valid GATE score followed by COAP counselling. Candidates must register on both the IIT Bombay admission portal and the COAP portal, after which seats are allotted through COAP counselling rounds. Candidates can check IIT Bombay–specific admission dates and COAP Counselling schedule in the table below.

Events Date

Seat Allocation Round 4 May 31 - Jun 03, 2026

IIT Bombay Fees 2026

IIT Bombay offers B.Tech with a total fee of INR 8,82,500. The details for IIT Bombay Courses & Fees are mentioned in the table below:

Course 1st Year Fee Total Fee
B.Tech INR 2,36,000 INR 8.83 Lakhs
M.Tech INR 72,000 INR 1.24 Lakhs
BS INR 2,36,000 INR 8.83 Lakhs
M.Sc INR 52,500 INR 84,500
B.Tech + M.Tech INR 2,36,000 INR 10.98 Lakhs
M.Des INR 72,000 INR 1.24 Lakhs
MBA INR 7,68,000 INR 15.16 Lakhs
PhD INR 89,200 INR 2.2 Lakhs

Ques. Is pursuing a B.Sc. at IIT Bombay a good investment for students?

Ans. As per B.Sc. students' and alumni feedback, the following are a few important points:

Benefits:

  • World-Class Infrastructure: The campus is equipped with up-to-date labs, libraries, and sports facilities.
  • Quality Education Provided: Experienced teachers focus on practical education.
  • Placed Well: Approximately 88% placement with the best offers up to INR 56 LPA and average packages of INR 18-20 LPA.
  • Scholarship Offered: Merit and income-based scholarships are available.
  • Project-Based Research: The curriculum has projects and interaction with Ph.D. researchers

Downsides:

  • Older Campus Facilities: A few hostels require renovation, and the sizes of rooms may be small for some.
  • Inconsistency in Teaching Quality: While most professors are superb, a few won't be as good.
  • Outdated Curriculum: Some subjects may not use the newest curriculum.

Overall Verdict:

Considering the strengths and limitations, an IIT Bombay B.Sc. is generally worth the investment. The institute offers high-quality education, world-class facilities, and strong placement assistance. Scholarships and research exposure are added advantages.

Improvement can be done in campus infrastructure and teaching practices. Despite the obstacles, the benefits of the program appear to far outweigh the demerits.

Ultimately, if a B.Sc. at IIT Bombay is "worth" it would be an individual decision based on personal goals and priorities. For those seeking a challenging academic environment, good career opportunities, and networking, IIT Bombay's B.Sc. program is a worthwhile option.

Ques. How is the MBA program structured at IIT Bombay?

Ans. IIT Bombay's two-year MBA program is broken up into eight terms. Students take 23 in-depth courses covering every facet of management during their first year. These classes expose students to the most recent practices in the industry through case studies, projects, and research assignments. Following the first two terms, students can also work on a one-month winter project.

At the end of the first year, the academic journey culminates in a summer project. Students can put their basic management knowledge to use through real-world, practical industry projects during this two-month project.

IIT Bombay Ranking

IIT Bombay is one of the top institutes for B.Tech in India. It is preferred mainly by top JEE (Advanced) rankers The institute has been ranked by NIRF, IIRF, India Today, The Week, Outlook, Times Higher Education, and QS World University rankings. IIT Bombay Admission 2026

IIT Bombay offers admission to various programs through national-level entrance exams, including JEE Advanced, GATE, CAT, etc. Admission to M.Tech is based on GATE, followed by COAP Counselling. Further, candidates have to register for COAP Counselling @gate.iisc.ac.in/coap2025 based on the GATE score.

The eligibility and selection criteria for the major courses are mentioned in the table below:

Course Eligibility Criteria Selection Criteria
B.Tech 10+2 with 75% marks in PCM JEE Advanced + JoSAA Counselling
M.Tech BE/B.Tech/B.Arch with 60% marks GATE + COAP Counselling
BS 10+2 with 75% marks in PCM JEE Advanced + JoSAA Counselling
M.Sc BS/B.Sc with 60% marks IIT JAM + JOAPS Counselling
MS by Research BE/B.Tech/BS with 60% marks GATE + COAP Counselling
B.Tech + M.Tech 10+2 with 75% marks in PCM JEE Advanced + JoSAA Counselling
B.Des 10+2 with 50% marks UCEED + Interview
M.Des B.Des/B.Arch/BE/B.Tech/BFA with 55% marks CEED + DAT + Interview
M.Des by Research B.Des/M.Des/M.Arch/MFA with 60% marks CEED + DAT + Interview
MBA Bachelor’s degree with 60% marks CAT + PI
PhD Master’s degree / Bachelor’s degree with 60% marks Written Test and/or Interview

Ques. Is pursuing an MBA from IIT Bombay a good choice?

Ans. The Shailesh J. Mehta School of Management at IIT Bombay offers the highly sought-after MBA program. The institute is well-regarded in the management field. To decide if IIT Bombay is the best place for your MBA, take into account these important factors:

  • Rankings: IIT Bombay has been ranked in the "Management" category by NIRF for the past three yeaINR It was listed as the tenth-best MBA school in India in 2024.
  • Placements: To assist MBA students in landing jobs at top companies, IIT Bombay organizes an annual placement drive. The highest package offered for the SJMSOM IIT Bombay MBA placements 2024 was INR 72 LPA, while the average package was INR 28.01 LPA. 48 companies visited the campus, with a 100% placement rate.
  • Faculty: The faculty members are highly qualified, with many having published extensively in reputable research journals, according to IIT Bombay Collegedunia Reviews.

Ques. What is the admission process for getting into IIT Bombay?

Ans. Admission to IIT Bombay is primarily through entrance exams (except for the EMBA program, which is based on merit and an interview). The admission process includes several steps: application submission, selection rounds, cutoff determination, merit list or counseling, among otheINR Candidates must pass the JEE Main and JEE Advanced exams in order to receive a B.Tech degree. Furthermore, candidates must pass exams like GATE, CAT, IIT JAM, UCEED, and CEED, respectively, to advance to the next admission stages for other programs like MTech, MBA, MSc, BDes, and MDes.

Ques. What are the steps to secure a full scholarship at IIT Bombay?

Ans. To earn a full scholarship at IIT Bombay, you must fulfill specific eligibility criteria. This includes having an excellent academic performance, showing remarkable skills and accomplishments in your study area, and proving financial need. Besides these, you might need to pass different rounds such as aptitude tests, interviews, and other selection processes. Having good grades is important, but so is showcasing your commitment to and potential in your field. Remember that there is fierce competition for scholarships at IIT Bombay, so you must perform exceptionally well in every way. However, the advantages can be genuinely life-changing if you're prepared to put in the effort and demonstrate your abilities. So, be determined to achieve your goals!

IIT Bombay Cutoff

The cutoff for different exams has not been released yet for the year 2026. IIT Bombay accepts JEE Advanced scores for admission to B.Tech, B.Tech + M.Tech, and BS courses and UCEED scores for B.Des admissions. Given below IIT Bombay Cutoff for B.Tech, M.Tech, and M.Sc programs for the General Category.

IIT Bombay JEE Advanced Cutoff 2025

IIT Bombay JEE Advanced Cutoff 2025 has been released. The Round 1 cutoff rank for B.Tech Computer Science Engineering in 2025 is 66, while in 2024, it was 68. For B.Tech Electrical Engineering, the cutoff rank has increased to 418 in 2025 from 464 in 2024.

IIT Bombay Placement

As per the IIT Bombay Placement report, the average package stood at INR 23.50 LPA, and the median package was INR 17.92 LPA. The institute secured a total of 1475 offers from top recruiters such as Apple, Google, Intel, Godrej, Halliburton, Accenture, Barclays, Michelin, Pfizer, Amazon, Honeywell, Hitachi, Boeing, Reliance, Sony, Qualcomm, Nvidia, Bosch, Texas Instruments, Adobe, and Atlassian.

The placement statistics for 2024, along with the comparison with the placement statistics of 2023, are mentioned below:

Particulars Placement Stats (2024) Placement Stats (2023)
No. of Registered Students 2414 2174
No. of Students Participated 1979 1845
No. of Companies Offered Jobs 364 324
No. of Job Offers 1650 1788
No. of Accepted Offers 1475 1516
Pre-Placement Offers Accepted 258 194
International Job Offers Accepted 78 65
Job Offers above INR 1 Cr PA 22 16
Average Package INR 23.50 LPA INR 21.82 LPA
Median Package INR 17.92 LPA INR 16.66 LPA

IIT Bombay vs IIT Delhi vs IIT Kharagpur

IIT Bombay, IIT Delhi, and IIT Kharagpur have been consistently ranked as the top institutes in India for B.Tech. Here is the comparison between these top three IITs of India:

Particulars IIT Bombay IIT Delhi IIT Kharagpur
Established Year 1958 1961 1951
NIRF Ranking (2024) 3rd for B.Tech, 3rd for Overall 2nd for B.Tech, 4th for Overall 5th for B.Tech, 6th for Overall
B.Tech 1st Year Fees INR 2,36,000 INR 2,28,650 INR 2,62,360
B.Tech Seat Intake 1,185 1,093 1,560
B.Tech Admission Criteria JEE Advanced + JoSAA Counselling JEE Advanced + JoSAA Counselling JEE Advanced + JoSAA Counselling
JEE Advanced Cutoff 2024 (General Category) 68 - 4716 116 - 5930 414 - 9906
Highest Package INR 2.2 Cr PA INR 2.4 Cr PA INR 2.14 Cr PA
Median Package INR 19.61 LPA INR 19.08 LPA INR 19.76 LPA

Ques. Which is the better choice for a B.Sc. program: IISc Bangalore or IIT Bombay?

Ans. When choosing between IIT Bombay and IISc Bangalore for a B.Sc. program, take into account aspects like tuition costs, compensation packages, and student evaluations. Here's a brief analogy:

Parameters B.Sc at IISc Bangalore B.Sc at IIT Bombay
Tuition Fee INR 40,000 INR 8 lakh
Median Salary INR 28 lakh INR 19.6 lakh
Student Rating 4.3/5 4.5/5

In conclusion, IIT Bombay has a marginally higher student rating than IISc Bangalore, but IISc Bangalore offers better affordability and Return on Investment (ROI) for a B.Sc. What matters most to you should guide your decision.

NOTE: Fee details are sourced from official data and may change. The student rating is an average given by B.Sc. students.

Ques. Is IIT Kharagpur or IIT Bombay better for pursuing an MSc in Chemistry?

Ans. Let's compare the MSc in Chemistry programs at IIT Kharagpur and IIT Bombay:

  1. Ranking: According to the Shiksha Survey, IIT Bombay is ranked higher for this course.
  2. Fees: The tuition fee at IIT Bombay is lower. It costs INR 30,000, compared to INR 82,600 at IIT Kharagpur.
  3. Infrastructure: IIT Bombay has superior infrastructure with modern classrooms, making it more student-friendly compared to IIT Kharagpur.
  4. Popularity: IIT Bombay is more popular, indicated by the higher IIT JAM Advanced cut-off scores. You need to be among the top 33 to get into its MSc Chemistry program.
  5. Placement: The average annual salary for graduates from IIT Bombay is INR 17.9 Lacs, whereas it's INR 10.3 Lacs for those from IIT Kharagpur.

Ques. Is IIT Delhi or IIT Bombay better for pursuing a B.Tech in Mechanical Engineering?

Ans. Both IIT Delhi and IIT Bombay offer Mechanical Engineering programs that are highly comparable to those at prestigious international universities. Both institutions provide first-rate infrastructure and facilities and have comparable QS rankings. While IIT Delhi is situated in the coveted Hauz Khas neighborhood, near the international airport and train stations, IIT Bombay boasts a larger campus with a lake. At both institutions, placement patterns and opportunities for additional study are essentially the same. There is not much of a difference, even though IIT Bombay typically has slightly higher program opening and closing ranks. While some students select IIT Delhi because of its close proximity to civil services preparation centers, others may select IIT Bombay due to its historical trends or regional considerations. In the end, the choice is based on individual preferences and local circumstances that could affect the person.

Ques. Should I choose IIT Bombay or NIFT Mumbai for a Bachelor of Design (BDes) program?

Ans. When deciding between IIT Bombay and NIFT Mumbai for a BDes program, consider these key factors:

Parameter IIT Bombay NIFT Mumbai
Total tuition fee* INR 8 Lacs - INR 11.6 lakh INR 5.6 Lacs - INR 11.7 lakh
IIRF Design Ranking 2024 4 20
Specialisation Industrial Design, Communication Design, Animation, etc. Fashion Design, Fashion Communication, Textile Design, etc.
Selection criteria UCEED NIFT Entrance Exam

*Note: Tuition fees are based on unofficial sources and may vary.

IIT Bombay Campus & Facilities

IIT Bombay campus is spread over 545 acres of land in Powai, amongst hills and Powai & Vihar lakes. Further, it is the first public college to have laid the foundation stone with assistance from UNESCO and has been declared an Institute of National Importance by the Parliament of India.

Highlights on major facilities offered at IIT Bombay are given below:

Library: IIT Bombay Central Library is a four-storied building with a carpet area of over 63,000 sft. and is located towards the North of the Main building. It has a total collection of 4.40 lakh items that comprises 2,53,504 Books, Theses, CDs & Videos, 21,403 E-Books, 12,203 TLL & BC (Book Bank) Collection, 1,19,576 Bound Volumes of Journals, 67,931 Reports, Pamphlets & Standards, 4,417 Photocopies, 37,00,000+ Journals (Print, Online) & 36,247 IITB Repositories. The library remains open from 9 AM to 11 PM on weekdays and 10 AM to 5 PM on weekends and holidays. The library has 12 servers, 45 PCs, and other accessories adequate to cater to the needs of useINR 6 PCs are meant for users to access OPAC, databases, e-books, e-journals, and other e-resources. The reading area in the library has been WI-Fi enabled to provide wireless access to the Internet.​

Sports: The institute caters to a wide range of sports activities like badminton, table tennis, chess, yoga, basketball, football, hockey, volleyball, and more for students for internal and external participation. These sports facilities are organized and overseen by students, but they are also open to faculty and staff membeINR

Event Venues: IITB has a variety of venues suitable for different purposes, including lectures, seminars, conferences, and personal events. Some of these venues include Victor Menezes Convention Center (VMCC), Lecture Halls, Auditoriums & venues for personal/official events. ​

IIT Bombay Hostels: IITB Campus comprises 17 hostels for students. Each hostel is an independent entity with its own mess facilities, recreational areas, etc. The hostel fee at IIT Bombay is INR 17,250.

Day Care: The institute also has a day-care facility for children between 2 to 12 years of age.

IIT Bombay

IIT Bombay Main Building

IIT Bombay Classrooms

IIT Bombay Auditorium

IIT Bombay Event

IIT Bombay Indoor Games Auditorium

Ques. What is the hostel life like at IIT Bombay?

Ans. IIT Bombay is well-known for its vibrant hostel life. The hostels are a popular option for students because of their well-maintained infrastructure and contemporary amenities. Numerous canteens and cafeterias offer reasonably priced food options. Numerous technical, athletic, and cultural events are held on campus. Students can play basketball, table tennis, pool, and a small football field in their free time. For students, the hostel experience at IIT Bombay is genuinely unforgettable due to the supportive environment.

Ques. Which institute offers better hostel facilities and amenities for BTech students, IIT Bombay or IIT Delhi?

Ans. According to feedback from students, IIT Delhi is often thought to have better hostel amenities than IIT Bombay. The hostels at IIT Delhi are commended for their cleanliness, spacious rooms, and well-maintained common areas. On campus, students also have access to a variety of sports facilities, gyms, and recreation areas. On the other hand, while IIT Bombay's hostels are generally good, they are sometimes noted for being crowded and lacking in some amenities. Nevertheless, IIT Bombay does offer a wide range of facilities, including a sports complex, a swimming pool, and a student activity center.

IIT Bombay FAQs

Ques. What are the scholarships offered by IIT Bombay?

Ans. IIT Bombay offers various scholarships including Merit Cum Means Scholarship (Institute Scholarship), Dr. Burjor Godrej Scholarship, Aditya Birla Scholarship, OPJEMS Scholarship, and INSPIRE Scholarship. The details for all the scholarships are mentioned below:

  • Merit Cum Means Scholarship (Institute Scholarship): Offered to candidates with an annual family income less than INR 4.5 lakhs on the basis of JEE Advanced rank. A free tuition fee of INR 25,000 per semester is awarded, along with a benefit of INR 1000 per month.
  • Aditya Birla Scholarship: A scholarship of INR 65,000 per annum is awarded to students who maintain a score of 7.0 and must be in the top 25% of their batch.
  • OPJEMS Scholarship: A scholarship of INR 65,000 per annum is awarded to the top 12 students from each batch from any specialization or stream for all 4 yeaINR
  • INSPIRE Scholarship: Students are offered a scholarship of INR 10,000 every year. The students must have opted for the Natural Sciences courses at the UG or PG level.

Ques. What is the hostel fee of IIT Bombay?

Ans. IIT Bombay has a residential campus with 17 hostels. Out of which 3 are female hostels, 13 are male hostels, and 1 co-ed hostel. Each hostel has its own mess facilities. The hostel fee is mentioned in the table below:

Particulars Fees Per Semester
Hostel Charges INR 8,300
Electricity and Water Charges INR 4,000
Hostel Amenities Fund INR 1,900
Hostel-Mess Security Deposit (One Time) INR 3,000
Total Hostel Fee INR 17,200
Per Semester Mess Advance INR 27,000
Total Fees Payable INR 44,200

Ques. How are the placements at IIT Bombay?

Ans. As per the placement report of 2024, IIT Bombay secured 1475 offers from 364 companies. The average package was INR 23.50 LPA, and the median package was INR 17.92 LPA. The research shows the highest package secured was INR 2.2 Cr PA, and 22 students accepted offers of above INR 1 Cr PA. INR 4 LPA was the lowest package secured by IIT Bombay. A total of 258 PPOs and 78 international offers were accepted by the students of batch 2024. The MBA batch of 2024 secured the highest package of INR 72 LPA and the average package stood at INR 28.01 LPA. 47% of MBA students received PPOs with the average package of INR 29.97 LPA.

Ques. Is IIT Bombay better than IIT Madras for B.Tech?

Ans. IIT Bombay and IIT Madras are both known for their B.Tech courses. However, the better option depends on factors like location, placements, fees, etc. IIT Bombay provides slightly better placement opportunities as it is the financial hub. However, IIT Madras is known for its better research opportunities. Students should choose IITB if they prioritize high placement packages, a strong alumni network, and a more diverse campus culture. Meanwhile, they should choose IITM if they are more interested in research, a strong academic program, a well-maintained campus, and a more affordable location.

Ques. How many seats are offered in the M.Tech program at IIT Bombay?

Ans. IIT Bombay offers a total of 938 seats for M.Tech under different specializations. The highest number of seats, ie, 131 seats, is offered for Electrical Engineering. 105 seats are available for Civil Engineering, and 100 seats are available for Mechanical Engineering. The institute offers 87 seats for Metallurgical Engineering, 86 seats for Computer Science Engineering, and 78 seats for Chemical Engineering.

Ques. How much is the fee for the MDes program at IIT Bombay?

Ans. The total tuition fee for the MDes program at IIT Bombay is approximately INR 1 Lakh, according to unofficial sources. SC, ST, PwD students, and children of institution staff are exempted from paying this fee. For General, EWS, and OBC-NCL candidates, there are two fee structures: concessional and non-concessional, mainly affecting the tuition fee. Besides tuition, students must also pay for exams, medical, and other fees. Students not living in hostels do not pay the hostel fee.

In addition to the course fees, a registration fee is applicable: INR 300 for General/OBC male candidates and INR 150 for SC/ST/PwD and female candidates.

Ques. How is the MBA program at IIT Bombay?

Ans. IIT Bombay offers MBA programs through its Shailesh J. Mehta School of Management (SJMSOM). MBA programs at IITB are offered under specializations such as Finance, Accounting, Operations, and HR. In addition, SJMSOM also provides technology management with core courses like Technology Policy, R&D Management, and Managing Technology Transfer. The first-year fee for MBA is INR 4,33,000 across all the specializations. SJMSOM of IIT Bombay has been ranked 10th by NIRF Ranking 2024 under the Management category and 5th by the QS World Ranking 2024 for Business & Management Studies. In terms of placement, the highest package secured in 2024 was INR 72 LPA, and the average package stood at INR 28.01 LPA.

Ques. Does IIT Bombay offer a student exchange program with international universities?

Ans. Yes, IIT Bombay has multiple student exchange programs with international universities. These programs are supported by various MoUs (Memorandums of Understanding) with institutions worldwide. Some of the universities involved include:

  • The University Paris-Sud 11, France
  • Universities de Liege, Belgium
  • Universities Catholique de Louvaine, Belgium
  • The Technische Universitat Darmstadt, Germany
  • Friedrich-Alexander Universitat Erlangen-Nurnberg, Germany

IIT Bombay: 209 Answered Questions

What is the average package of a civil engineer in IIT Bombay?

Manish Soni

IIT Bombay Civil Engineering placements — honestly one of the most misunderstood departments, so let me give you the actual data and context.

2024 placement data for Civil Engineering at IIT Bombay:

  • Average CTC: approximately ?10-13 LPA (significantly lower than CSE's ?18-22 LPA).
  • Highest domestic: ~?40-50 LPA (consulting, finance). International: ?80-90 LPA.
  • Placement rate: ~85-88%.

Career paths and companies:

  • Core civil roles: L&T, AECOM, Bechtel, Jacobs Engineering (~?7-11 LPA starting).
  • PSUs via GATE: IOCL, NTPC, ONGC, NHAI — strong pathway for IIT Bombay Civil grads. PSU salary: ?8-12 LPA + excellent benefits.
  • Consulting/Analytics: McKinsey, BCG, Bain (3-4 offers annually). IIT brand matters here over branch. ~?20-30 LPA.
  • Finance: Some pivot to IB/PE via CAT or directly. 10-15% of civil grads take MBA after 2-3 years.
  • Higher studies: MS at MIT, Stanford, ETH Zurich — IIT Bombay Civil grads are very competitive for top MS programs internationally.

Reality most don't say: ~35-40% of IIT Bombay Civil students do NOT go into core civil roles long-term. The IIT brand is portable — but you need to actively position yourself for non-core if that's your goal. Core civil from IIT Bombay is genuinely rewarding if you're passionate about infrastructure — the ?10-13 LPA starting is much higher than private civil engineering college grads.

Which is better, IISc or IIT Bombay?

Pooja Iyer

IISc Bangalore vs IIT Bombay — two of India's finest institutions but quite different:

IISc (Indian Institute of Science, Bangalore):

  • India's #1 research institution (QS, NIRF). world-class for basic sciences and engineering research
  • primarily postgraduate (MTech, ME, PhD) — undergraduate is the 4-year BS research program
  • for research careers: IISc is unmatched in India. alumni in top global research labs, faculty positions worldwide
  • not the right choice if you want a corporate job after 4 years — the BS program is research-oriented

IIT Bombay:

  • India's top engineering institution (alongside IIT Madras) for technology and industry-facing careers
  • excellent placements in tech, finance, consulting. placement is a major strength
  • strong research too, but more balanced between research and industry compared to IISc
  • BTech programs are primarily engineering-focused

simple guideline: if you want a career in fundamental research, PhD, or academic science — IISc. if you want a technology/industry career with strong placement and brand — IIT Bombay. both are world-class institutions in their domains.

Can I get into IIT Bombay with Rank 3000 in JEE Advanced?

Ambika Das

3000 JEE Advanced rank for IIT Bombay — be realistic about this:

IIT Bombay is the most competitive IIT and at 3000 general rank, most flagship programs are beyond reach:

  • CSE at IIT Bombay typically closes around rank 50-100 for general. far out of reach at 3000
  • EE, Mech, Chemical, Civil — all close in the 300-1200 range for general. not accessible at 3000
  • what might open at IIT Bombay at 3000 general: Energy Science & Engineering, Metallurgical, some interdisciplinary programs in later rounds. these close in the 2,500–4,000+ range

realistic options at 3000 JEE Advanced general:

  • IIT Delhi — Maths & Computing, Engineering Physics might just open
  • IIT Roorkee — ECE, EE, Chemical available
  • IIT Kharagpur — several branches open
  • IIT Guwahati — CSE, ECE available

to get IIT Bombay's main branches at 3000 rank you'd need to be OBC (category rank ~900-1200 for CSE) or SC/ST. if you're general category, check JoSAA closing ranks from 2024 for IIT Bombay specifically — you might get some interdisciplinary programs but not core engineering. 3000 general is IIT Roorkee/Kharagpur level for good branches, not IIT Bombay mainstream.

Is 12,000 JEE Advanced Rank decent enough for admission into an IIT?

Santosh Kumar

yes, 12,000 JEE Advanced rank is enough to get into an IIT — but branch and campus choices are limited. total IIT seats are 17,385+ so you're well within range.

what's realistic at 12,000 AIR (general category):

  • newer IITs (IIT Bhilai, Goa, Jammu, Dharwad, Tirupati, Palakkad) — can get CSE, ECE, or EE at most of these. these campuses are 6-10 years old, infrastructure is still developing but faculty quality is solid
  • older IITs (BHU, Dhanbad/ISM) — non-core branches like Chemical, Metallurgy, Mining, Bioengineering might open up. CSE/ECE won't
  • IIT Bombay, Delhi, Madras, Kanpur, Kharagpur, Roorkee, Guwahati — basically not happening at 12,000 general

important thing most people miss — your category rank matters more than AIR. if you're OBC/SC/ST, a 12,000 AIR can unlock significantly better IITs and branches. check your category rank separately. also, newer IITs like Bhilai and Goa have been steadily improving placements and research output — don't write them off just because they're newer.

Why is getting into IIT Bombay tougher than getting into Harvard?

Neelam Joshi

IIT Bombay vs Harvard — why does people say IIT is harder to get into?

it's about acceptance rates relative to applicants:

  • Harvard: ~3.5% acceptance rate from ~57,000 applicants = ~2,000 students admitted per year
  • IIT Bombay CSE (and top IITs overall): approximately 0.02% of JEE Main aspirants get the top IIT branches. About 13 lakh students appear for JEE Main; ~1.7 lakh qualify for JEE Advanced; roughly 16,000 get IITs — that's about 1.2% of all JEE Main takers. For CSE at IIT Bombay specifically: ~150 seats for 13+ lakh JEE Main takers = 0.01%

so on pure acceptance rate math for the top programs, IIT Bombay CSE is more selective than Harvard's overall admit rate.

however the comparison isn't fully apples-to-apples: Harvard selects holistically (essays, recommendations, extracurriculars) while IIT is purely exam-based. Harvard's applicant pool is global and self-selected; JEE has near-universal participation from Indian 12th-grade science students.

different systems, different difficulty types. but the math of selection ratios supports the claim.

What is the CSE syllabus of B.Tech at IIT Bombay?

Saurabh Nanda

IIT Bombay BTech CSE syllabus overview (broad structure):

Year 1-2 (Foundation): Mathematics (Calculus, Linear Algebra, Probability & Statistics), Introduction to Computer Programming (Python/C), Digital Logic Design, Discrete Math, Computer Organization

Year 2-3 (Core CS): Data Structures & Algorithms, Design & Analysis of Algorithms, Operating Systems, Computer Networks, Database Systems, Theory of Computation, Software Engineering, Logic Design

Year 3-4 (Advanced + Electives): Machine Learning, Artificial Intelligence, Computer Vision, Natural Language Processing, Distributed Systems, Compiler Design, Cryptography, Advanced Algorithms — plus research projects and electives across departments

Final Year: BTP (B.Tech Project) — a research/implementation project. many IIT Bombay students use this for research papers or startup prototyping.

IIT Bombay CSE is one of India's most rigorous programs. the curriculum is heavily research-oriented with strong focus on theory + systems + applied areas. check cse.iitb.ac.in for the full current syllabus — it gets updated periodically.

How do the placements really compare between IIT Delhi B.Tech Computer Science Engineering and IIT Bombay B.Tech Mechanical Engineering?

AstralApricotTart

IIT Delhi's B.Tech in Computer Science offers a strong ROI with high internship opportunities from top companies like Amazon and Google, despite the higher fee structure of around 20 lakhs. This investment can pay off well in the tech field, which is booming. In contrast, IIT Bombay's B.Tech in Mechanical Engineering has a similar fee but may not deliver the same level of placement offers, especially given the current economic climate.

If you’re inclined towards tech and want to maximize your job prospects, IIT Delhi is the smarter choice for 2025. However, if you have a passion for mechanical engineering and the broader opportunities it provides, IIT Bombay is still a solid option.

Which one should I opt for IIT Delhi B.Tech Civil Engineering or IIT Bombay B.Tech Computer Science Engineering

Echolocation

IIT Bombay's B.Tech in Computer Science Engineering offers a significant edge in placements and internship opportunities compared to IIT Delhi's B.Tech in Civil Engineering. IIT Bombay attracts top tech firms like Google, Amazon, and Microsoft, with students often receiving higher packages, including offers that can exceed 2.65 crore. On the other hand, while IIT Delhi's civil engineering program has its strengths, the placement scenarios are generally less lucrative, especially in tech-driven roles. If you're leaning towards a tech career with better job prospects and packages, IIT Bombay is the clear choice; however, if your passion lies in civil engineering and you're less concerned about tech salaries, IIT Delhi is a solid option.

However, keep in mind that Electrical Engineering (Power) is a very challenging branch!

How do the placements really compare between IIT Delhi B.Tech Civil Engineering and IIT Bombay B.Tech Computer Science Engineering?

LucidLooter

IIT Delhi's B.Tech in Civil Engineering offers a significantly lower tuition fee compared to IIT Bombay's B.Tech in Computer Science, which typically ranges much higher. While IIT Delhi students can expect a total cost of around 4-5 lakhs due to tuition exemptions, IIT Bombay's costs can soar to 12-13 lakhs. In terms of placements, IIT Bombay's Computer Science program has the edge with top tech giants and higher salary packages, making it a better ROI for students aiming for lucrative careers in tech. If you’re passionate about civil engineering and want a cost-effective education, IIT Delhi is a great choice. However, if you're inclined towards a high-paying tech career and can manage the higher fees, IIT Bombay is likely the smarter financial investment.

S
Sweta Kumari
Jan 13, 2026 20:11

IIT Bombay admission is not possible without qualifying for JEE Advanced.

  • Since you are in Class 12 and appearing for JEE Main 2026 (January attempt), your path is:
  • Qualify JEE Main 2026 with a rank within the cutoff.
  • Become eligible for JEE Advanced 2026.
  • Appear and clear JEE Advanced 2026 with a high rank.
  • Participate in JoSAA counselling for IIT Bombay.

Sweta Kumari
Jan 20, 2026 17:29

You cannot get direct admission to IIT Bombay without appearing in the required entrance exams. For B.Tech, admission is through JEE Advanced after qualifying JEE Main. For M.Tech, MBA, or PhD, respective exams like GATE, JAM, CAT, or institute-specific tests are mandatory. An entrance qualification is essential. The fees for the courses are mentioned below:

Course Total Fees Eligibility

M.Tech

 INR 1.24 Lakhs Pass in Graduation + GATE

B.Tech

 INR 8.83 Lakhs 10+2 with 75% + JEE Advanced

M.Sc

 INR 84,500 Graduation + JAM

BS

 INR 8.83 Lakhs 10+2 + JEE

Ph.D

 INR 2.2 Lakhs Post Graduation

M.Des

 INR 1.24 Lakhs Graduation with 55% + CEED

B.Tech + M.Tech

 INR 10.98 Lakhs 10+2 with 75% + JEE Advanced

Master of Business Administration [MBA]

 INR 15.16 Lakhs Graduation with 60% + CAT

B.Des

 INR 8.83 Lakhs Pass in 10+2 + UCEED

M.Sc

 INR 1.24 Lakhs Graduation + GATE

Sweta Kumari
Apr 1, 2026 23:42

IIT Bombay Admission process is conducted in online mode. Candidates have to appear for the exams such as JEE Advanced, GATE, CEED, CAT, and IIT JAM. Based on the entrance score, candidates have to register for JoSAA Counselling for B.Tech, B.Tech + M.Tech, and BS admissions. For M.Tech and MS by Research admissions, register for COAP Counselling, and for M.Sc, register for JOAPS Counselling.

Selection for PhD courses will be based on the Written Test and/or Personal Interview conducted by the institute. For PhD applications, students have to register @iitb.ac.in/newacadhome/phd.jsp. The application fee is INR 300 for General/EWS/OBC candidates and INR 150 for Women/SC/ST/PwD candidates.

Sweta Kumari
Apr 22, 2026 23:27

Admission to Indian Institute of Technology Bombay is through JEE Advanced after qualifying JEE Main. Candidates must register for JoSAA counselling, fill choices of branches, and participate in seat allotment rounds. Final admission is confirmed after document verification, fee payment, and meeting cutoff rank requirements for selected programs.

Sweta Kumari
May 30, 2026 21:43

Admission to Indian Institute of Technology Bombay is based on national entrance examinations. For B.Tech courses, students must qualify JEE Main and JEE Advanced, followed by JoSAA counselling. For M.Tech, MBA, M.Sc, and Ph.D. programs, admissions are through exams like GATE, CAT, JAM, or institute-level selection processes, including interviews and document verification.

S
shikhar pathak
Jan 19, 2025 20:32
HOW TO GIT ADDMISSON IN IIT
FollowReply
S
Sweta Kumari
Jan 23, 2025 22:25

The selection rounds of IIT Bombay involve entrance exams, counselling rounds, written tests and interviews. 

  • Among all the courses, the institute offers a BTech program as its one of the most opted course for 4 years. Admission to this program is done through the JEE Advanced scores followed by the JoSAA counselling. Candidates can appear for the JEE Advanced examination upon clearing the JEE Mains exam. 
  • IIT Bombay M.Tech, M.Sc and MBA admission to is made through the GATE, IIT JAM, and CAT examinations respectively.
  • The institute also offers admission to other PG courses like MDes, EMBA, MDP, and MPP.
  • IIT Bombay also provides students with some integrated courses like MA+PhD, MTech+PhD, and MSc+PhD.
  • IIT Bombay offers admission to Ph.D. courses based on merit in the written examination followed by an interview.

IIT Bombay has reserved 15% of seats for SC applicants and 7.5% for ST students. Additionally, 27% of seats are reserved for OBC applicants. Candidates can apply online to the official website for their preferred course.

N
nidhee ramadhar shukla
Oct 24, 2024 20:35
I need information about IIT Bombay college
FollowReply
S
Sweta Kumari
Oct 26, 2024 00:34

Established in 1958, Indian Institute of Technology Bombay popularly known as IIT Bombay or IIT Mumbai is an autonomous institute and deemed university located in the Powai region of Bombay. IIT Bombay is the most preferred IIT because 75% of JEE Advanced top rankers opt for IIT Bombay for its excellent placement records. IIT Mumbai is recognized by UGC & AICTE. According to the NIRF Ranking 2023, IITB is ranked 3rd in the Engineering category. Internationally, IIT Bombay is ranked 149 by the QS World University Ranking 2024 under the Top Global Universities.

How many branches are there
FollowReply
A
Ashish Ramtari
Aug 28, 2024 11:34

There are many branches available in IIT Bombay at UG and PG levels. Given below are the major branches offered in the institute:

  • Computer Science
  • Biotechnology
  • Mathematics
  • Economics
  • Aerospace Engineering
  • Chemistry
  • Animation and Multimedia
  • Statistics
